zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Robert de Ferrers 6th Earl of Derby於1239年出生在英國斯塔福爾圖特波利時，他的父親Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby47歲，母親Margaret de Quincy Countess of Derby21歲。他於1249年與Marie de Lusignan在英國英格蘭倫敦維斯特明斯特維斯特明斯特 艾比結婚。他於1266年登記服兵役。他去世於1279年4月在英國斯塔福爾哈特利 卡素享年40歲，埋葬在英國斯塔福爾斯塔福德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ferrers
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: locative name from Ferrières-Saint-Hilaire (Eure) and Ferrières (Manche).

variants.name: Ferris
meanings: Irish and Scottish: shortened Anglicized form of Irish Ó Fearghuis or Ó Fearghasa ‘descendant of Fearghus’, or from the Scottish-Gaelic form of this personal name, Fearghus (see Fergus ).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Ferrières-Saint-Hilaire, Eure and Ferrières, La Manche in Normandy, France.

variants.name: Ferridge
meanings: English: unexplained; possibly a form of Ferris and therefore a variant of Ferrers .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert de Ferrers 6th Earl of Derby was born in 1239, in Tutbury, Staffordshire, England, his father, Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby, was 47 and his mother, Margaret de Quincy Countess of Derby, was 21. He married Marie de Lusignan in 1249, in Westminster Abbey, Westminster, London, England, United Kingdom. He registered for military service in 1266. He died in April 1279, in Chartley Castle, Staffordshire, England, at the age of 40, and was buried in Stafford, Staffordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Robert de Ferrers 6th Earl of Derby est né en 1239, à Tutbury, Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby, avait 47 ans et sa mère, Margaret de Quincy Countess of Derby, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Marie de Lusignan en 1249, à Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1266. Il est décédé en avril 1279, à Chartley Castle, Staffordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 40 ans, et a été enterré à Stafford, Staffordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Robert de Ferrers 6th Earl of Derby 1239 in Tutbury, Staffordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby 47 und seine Mutter Margaret de Quincy Countess of Derby 21. Er hat 1249 in Westminster Abbey, Westminster, London, England, United Kingdom Marie de Lusignan geheiratet. Er hat sich 1266 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb im April 1279 im Alter von 40 Jahren in Chartley Castle, Staffordshire, England und wurde in Stafford, Staffordshire, England begraben.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert de Ferrers 6th Earl of Derby(이)가 1239년 영국 스태퍼드실르 튜트버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby(은)는 47세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret de Quincy Countess of Derby(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1249년 영국 잉글랜드 런던 웨스트민스터 웨스트민스터 애브브이에서 Marie de Lusignan(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1266년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1279년 4월 영국 스태퍼드실르 처틀리 카스틀에서 40세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스태퍼드실르 스태퍼드에 묻혔다.

ru.Westminster Abbey, London, England: вестминстир эбби, вестминстир, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Robert de Ferrers 6th Earl of Derby родился в 1239 г. в местности тутбури, стаффордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Quincy Countess of Derby, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Marie de Lusignan в 1249 г. в местности вестминстир эбби, вестминстир, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания. В 1266 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер апрель 1279 г. в местности чартли кастл, стаффордшир, Англия в возрасте 40 лет и был похоронен в местности стаффорд, стаффордшир, Англия.

Se casó con Marie de Lusignan en 1249, en Westminster Abbey, Westminster, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1266. Murió en abril de 1279, en Chartley Castle, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 40 años, y fue sepultado en Stafford, Staffordshire, Inglaterra.
